meta.title: HYROX AI agent training program
meta.design_goal: Feasible progression from casual desk-based starter to elite HYROX athlete while preserving safety, adherence, and race specificity.
evidence_basis: Official HYROX race format and division weights
HYROX preparation guidance: running, movement standards, pacing, rest, food, and hydration
Frontiers in Physiology 2025 HYROX study: hard to very hard event intensity, running-dominant time distribution, VO2max and endurance-volume association, and need for compromised running
ACSM and CDC general adult aerobic plus strength minimums
safety: This is training guidance, not medical advice.
For inactive users, start at casual level and keep at least two low-intensity weeks before hard intervals.
Pain that changes gait, chest pain, fainting, acute swelling, or unusual breathlessness overrides the plan.
Increase total weekly training load by small steps and deload every fourth week.

casual.label: Casual starter
casual.best_for: Desk-based beginners, casual gamers, relay athletes, or first-time Open/Doubles finishers.
entry_checks: Can complete bodyweight squat, hinge, plank, and light carry without pain
casual.success_target: Finish healthy, understand every station, and keep run-walk rhythm under fatigue.

recreational.label: Recreational Open
recreational.best_for: Active gym users or runners aiming for a strong Open or Doubles result.
entry_checks: Can train 4 days weekly for 4 weeks
Can safely move light sled, carries, lunges, row, and SkiErg
recreational.success_target: Strong Open finish with controlled pacing and no station breakdown.

competitive.label: Competitive age-group
competitive.best_for: Experienced hybrid athletes targeting age-group competitiveness, Open PB, or first Pro attempt.
entry_checks: Can complete race-standard Open loads safely
Can recover from two quality sessions weekly
competitive.success_target: Run consistently after stations, minimize transition losses, and approach Pro-load readiness.

elite.label: Elite pathway
elite.best_for: Advanced racers with coach oversight, Pro-load competence, and results-driven goals.
entry_checks: Can complete Pro loads to standard
Has recent race, PFT, or time-trial benchmarks
Has a coach or experienced reviewer for technique and recovery
elite.success_target: Elite-style pacing, high compromised-running economy, and qualification-oriented benchmarking.

foundation.label: Foundation
foundation.purpose: Build aerobic base, movement literacy, basic strength, tissue tolerance, and repeatable training rhythm.
foundation.progression: Most work stays easy. Add one short controlled quality exposure weekly after week 2.

build.label: Build
build.purpose: Add threshold running, station strength endurance, and controlled compromised running.
build.progression: Two quality days weekly with one station-density session and one run-quality session.

specific.label: Race specific
specific.purpose: Practice 1 km repeats under station fatigue, transitions, station standards, and goal race pacing.
specific.progression: One race-pace brick and one longer simulation weekly, scaled by level.

peak.label: Peak and taper
peak.purpose: Keep sharpness, reduce fatigue, confirm standards, and arrive fresh.
peak.progression: Reduce volume 30 to 50 percent while keeping short race-pace touches.

monday.title: Reset mobility and aerobic walk
main: Easy walk, bike, or jog in conversational effort
10 min station-standard review: squat depth, lunge step, wall-ball target line
notes: If Sunday left soreness above 6/10, make today mobility only.
Elite athletes may add an easy second aerobic session only if sleep and soreness are good.

tuesday.title: Easy run plus movement standards
main: Easy run or run-walk
Then 3 rounds smooth: 10 air squats, 8 light kettlebell deadlifts, 6 inchworms
cooldown: Record average heart rate, RPE, and any niggles
notes: Casual level may use 3 min jog / 2 min walk.
Do not chase pace yet; build repeatability.

wednesday.title: Strength base: squat, hinge, push, pull, carry
warmup: 2 rounds: 8 lunges, 8 glute bridges, 8 scap push-ups
main: Goblet squat or front squat 3-5 x 6
Romanian deadlift 3-5 x 6
Push-up or bench press 3-4 x 8
Row or pull-up variation 3-4 x 8
Farmers carry 4-8 x 30 m
notes: Casual level uses lighter loads and perfect reps.
Competitive and elite can finish with 4 x 20 m low sled push if technique is clean.

thursday.title: Erg aerobic intervals
main: 6-10 x 2 min steady erg / 1 min easy
Alternate row and SkiErg if both are available
notes: Keep stroke mechanics smooth.
This builds aerobic work without adding running impact.

friday.title: Rest or easy mobility
main: Mobility for calves, hips, thoracic spine, wrists
Optional 15-25 min zone 1 bike
notes: Do not turn today into conditioning.
If resting heart rate is elevated, keep it complete rest.

saturday.title: Intro compromised running
warmup: Movement prep for lunges, burpees, carries
main: 3-6 rounds: 400-800 m easy run, then one station skill at controlled effort
Rotate station skills: SkiErg, row, light carry, light sandbag lunge, wall-ball mechanics
cooldown: Write down which station disrupted running most
notes: This is not a race simulation yet.
Stop a station set before technique breaks.

sunday.title: Long easy aerobic
main: Continuous easy run, run-walk, bike, or mixed aerobic session
Keep the last 10 min as easy as the first 10 min
notes: The goal is time on feet, not speed.
Casual athletes can split into two shorter walks.

monday.title: Rest and readiness check
main: Mobility plus readiness score: sleep, soreness, mood, resting heart rate if available
cooldown: Plan the week and confirm equipment access
notes: If readiness is poor, reduce Tuesday intensity one step.

tuesday.title: Threshold 1 km rhythm
main: 3-8 x 1 km at controlled threshold with 2 min easy jog or walk
Finish with 2 sets of 15-30 wall balls or wall-ball substitutes
notes: Casual athletes use 4 x 3 min instead of full 1 km repeats.
Elite athletes may add a second easy aerobic session later in the day.

wednesday.title: Sled and lower-body strength endurance
warmup: Sled marching drills or hill walk
main: Sled push technique 4-8 x 12.5-25 m
Sled pull technique 4-8 x 12.5-25 m
Sandbag lunge 3-5 x 20-40 m
Single-leg accessory 2-4 sets
notes: No sled? Use hill pushes, heavy prowler substitute, backward drags, and loaded step-ups.
Load should never force unsafe spine position.

thursday.title: Easy run and erg technique
main: Easy run or run-walk
Then 10-15 min alternating SkiErg and row technique
notes: This should make Saturday better, not worse.

friday.title: Station density circuit
warmup: Movement standards for burpees, carries, lunges
main: 3-6 rounds quality: 250 m SkiErg, 20 m carry, 10 burpee broad jumps, 250 m row, 20 m sandbag lunge
Rest enough to keep mechanics and breathing controlled
notes: Do not add race pace running today if Saturday is the main brick.
Elite athletes can keep rests short but not sloppy.

saturday.title: Compromised run builder
main: 4-8 rounds: 800-1000 m run, then one station segment at planned race effort
Use a different station each round and record post-station run pace
cooldown: Carbs, fluids, and sodium if session exceeds 75 min
notes: This session teaches pace control after fatigue.
If run pace falls apart early, reduce station load before adding more running.

sunday.title: Long easy plus mobility
main: Long easy run, run-walk, or bike-run blend
Last 10 min includes relaxed cadence focus
notes: If Saturday was very hard, swap this for bike or walk.

monday.title: Recovery and race-standard audit
main: Mobility and video review of last brick
Check station standards that caused no-rep risk
cooldown: Schedule equipment and race simulation details
notes: A standard audit saves more time than another hard workout.

tuesday.title: Race-pace 1 km repeats
main: 4-8 x 1 km at goal HYROX run pace with 90-120 sec easy recovery
After every second repeat: 15 wall balls or 100 m row easy-moderate
notes: Goal race pace should feel controlled early.
If every rep is a sprint, the target is too aggressive.

wednesday.title: Race-load station endurance
main: Sled push and pull at race or scaled load
Farmers carry and sandbag lunge at race or scaled load
Wall-ball sets using planned race break strategy
notes: The objective is standards under fatigue, not max strength.
Casual athletes stay scaled and prioritize continuous movement.

thursday.title: Easy aerobic and mobility
main: Easy run, bike, row, or SkiErg
10 min transition practice: enter station calmly, set equipment, first rep smooth
notes: Keep this genuinely easy so Friday and Saturday are useful.

friday.title: Controlled HYROX brick
main: Half to three-quarter HYROX: 4-6 x 1 km run plus 4-6 station segments
Use planned race pace, planned transitions, and planned wall-ball break strategy
cooldown: Write exact splits and station notes
notes: Casual athletes do a half simulation.
Elite athletes may alternate race-pace and faster-than-race station segments.

saturday.title: Long aerobic or full simulation slot
main: Normal week: long easy aerobic with 4 x 20 sec strides
Simulation week: scaled or full HYROX rehearsal no closer than 14 days from race day
notes: Full simulations are optional for beginners and must not wreck the next week.
Elite athletes should use this slot for race modeling only when recovery is strong.

sunday.title: Rest and recovery score
main: Mobility, food prep, sleep planning, and next-week load decision
notes: If soreness is still high by evening, reduce Monday and Tuesday.

monday.title: Rest and taper check
main: No conditioning unless it improves readiness
Confirm race date, start time, kit, shoes, and nutrition
notes: Fresh beats fit-but-fatigued in peak week.

tuesday.title: Race-pace sharpening
main: 3-5 x 1 km at goal race pace with generous recovery
Then 2 easy station touches: row or SkiErg plus wall-ball mechanics
notes: Finish feeling like one more rep was possible.

wednesday.title: Light strength and standards
main: 2-3 light sets each: squat, hinge, push, pull, carry
Light wall balls and lunges to confirm rhythm
notes: No heavy eccentric work.
No new shoes, grips, or station technique experiments.

thursday.title: Easy aerobic with strides
main: Easy run or bike
4 x 15-20 sec relaxed strides if legs feel good
notes: Skip strides if they create tightness.

friday.title: Rest, race logistics, and fueling
main: Pack kit, confirm transport, start wave, nutrition, hydration, warm-up area, and toilet timing
Visualize first 2 km controlled
notes: Do not test fitness today.

saturday.title: Race primer or race day
main: If race tomorrow: 2 rounds easy: 200 m run, 8 wall balls, 100 m row or SkiErg
If race today: use warm-up, pacing, transition, and fueling notes instead
notes: Primer should reduce anxiety and sharpen rhythm, not create fatigue.

sunday.title: Post-race recovery or full rest
main: Post-race: walk, hydrate, protein plus carbs, note splits while memory is fresh
No race: full rest and readiness check
notes: Do not judge the next training block until soreness and emotions settle.

race_day_notes: Start the first 1 km below ego pace; HYROX rewards even effort.
Use planned station break strategies before failure, especially wall balls.
Keep transitions boring: know where to enter, breathe before first rep, leave promptly.
Fuel and hydration should be rehearsed in training, not invented on race day.
If a judge calls no-reps, slow down and fix the standard immediately.
